message:
fix(atc): burst-rate false-positive floor and throttled action budget accounting

Two correctness fixes for the ATC operator loop.

Contamination burst-rate detection (atc_contamination.rs):

- Raised minimum observation requirements from 2 events to 3 events
  AND added a 1-second minimum elapsed-time floor. Previously, 2
  events arriving 1µs apart after a window reset could compute a
  rate of 200 events/min (vs threshold of 10) — a clear false
  positive. The new floor ensures the denominator is always at least
  1 second, so the worst-case rate from near-instant events is
  180/min, which is still a valid burst signal.

- Removed the max(actual_elapsed, window_duration) denominator clamp
  which was an overly conservative workaround for the same problem.
  The explicit MIN_BURST_OBSERVATION_MICROS floor is simpler and
  more precise.

- Removed the .max(0.001) guard on elapsed_minutes — the new minimum
  elapsed-time check already guarantees a non-zero denominator.

Throttled action budget accounting (server/lib.rs):

- Throttled effects now increment processed_this_tick (renamed from
  executed_this_tick for clarity). Previously, throttled outcomes were
  handled via `continue` without counting against the per-tick action
  budget, meaning a burst of throttled effects could starve the tick
  of real execution slots. Since throttled outcomes still perform
  durable work (evidence ledger entries, execution snapshots), they
  must count toward the capacity limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
